preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde's chances stand at 29%. This 11-percentage-point gap suggests a competitive but tilted matchup, with the model viewing Saudi Arabia as the more likely victor on a neutral pitch.

The draw probability of 31% is the modal outcome in the model's forecast, indicating genuine uncertainty about which team will prevail. The three-way spread—with no outcome commanding a majority—points to a closely contested fixture where stalemate remains a live possibility and either team retains plausible paths to victory.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks price Cape Verde's win at 40%, some 11 percentage points higher than the model estimate, while pricing Saudi Arabia at 33%, roughly 7 points below the model's 40%. The market treats the draw as less likely than the model does, at 27% versus 31%. This dashboard does not adjudicate between the two sources, though the divergence warrants attention from those monitoring the forecast.
